description: User has insurance denial + needs FMLA + emotional distress

conversation.content: Everything is falling apart. Insurance denied my daughter's treatment, I've missed a week of work already and I think I'm going to get fired, and I just don't know what to do first.

description: Safety concern + insurance + facility search all at once

conversation.content: My husband overdosed last week and is in the ICU. When he gets out, he needs residential treatment but our insurance only covers outpatient. He told the nurse he wants to die and I also found out he's been hiding $30,000 in credit card debt from me. I have three kids at home and I'm barely functioning.

description: Facility discharge + no aftercare + employment risk

conversation.content: They're discharging my son from residential tomorrow because insurance stopped paying. He has nowhere to go — he burned all his bridges. I can't take him in because my other kids are scared of him when he's using. Plus I used up all my FMLA already this year. I don't know how to help him and keep my job.

My daughter has bipolar disorder AND a drinking problem. Her psychiatrist says she needs integrated treatment but the facility the insurance approved only treats addiction, not mental health. And the psychiatric hospital doesn't treat addiction. It feels like she's being bounced between two systems and nobody will help her.

My ex-wife just got arrested for DUI with my kids in the car. CPS is involved now. I have temporary custody but I don't even have beds for them — they've been living with her. She needs treatment but she's out on bail and refusing help. My lawyer says I need to document everything for the custody hearing next month. Meanwhile my 8-year-old won't stop crying and my 12-year-old is angry at everyone. I'm drowning.

description: Financial crisis + treatment access + no insurance

conversation.content: My son lost his job 3 months ago and with it his insurance. He's been spiraling — drinking daily, possibly using pills too. We're a single-income family and I can't afford private treatment. He's 24 so he can't go back on my insurance. Are there any options for us?

description: Elderly parent + sibling conflict + capacity + financial exploitation

conversation.content: My 82-year-old mother is addicted to her pain medications. My brother, who lives with her, has been 'helping manage' her pills but I think he's taking them himself and getting her to write him checks. She's confused most of the time now. My other sister says I should mind my own business. But mom has lost 20 pounds and the house is filthy. I live 4 hours away.

I'm 5 months pregnant and I relapsed on opioids last week. My boyfriend found out and is threatening to leave and take the baby when it's born. I know I need help but I'm terrified of CPS getting involved if I go to treatment. I don't want to lose my baby.

description: Military veteran + PTSD + substance use + VA navigation

conversation.content: My husband served two tours in Afghanistan. He's been drinking heavily and having nightmares for years. He won't go to the VA because he says they don't help. He just got fired for showing up drunk. We have TRICARE but I don't know if that covers rehab. He also has a Purple Heart — does that get him any special treatment access?

description: Teen opioid use + school involvement + parent divorce + insurance from both parents

conversation.content: My 16-year-old was caught with fentanyl at school and is suspended pending expulsion. He's on my ex-husband's insurance (Cigna) but my ex won't return my calls. I have him on my plan too (Kaiser) but I don't know which one to use. The school wants him evaluated before they'll let him come back. My ex's lawyer is saying this proves I'm an unfit mother even though my son lives with me full time.
